what_it_does: A React surface that fetches a batch of proposed registry dossiers from GET /api/registry/proposals, lets an operator skip individual entries, and commits the kept set with a single POST to /api/registry/approve.
observations: The file opens with a dense interpretive header framing it as RG8, the proposal-batch surface, and states design laws: operator-only floor, no-fiction visibility, plain-language rendering, shared kit usage, and reflects-never-owns.
It defines typed schemas RegistryHowto, RegistryDossier, and ProposalBatch that mirror the register_element output contract.
useRegistryProposals is a self-contained hook that fetches the batch, treats a 404 as an honest empty state, surfaces other errors, and exposes an approve handler.
FeatureTag and GroundingTag render small badges that expose maps_to_feature and grounding state to the operator.
DossierCard renders each dossier in plain language (what/what_you_can_do/how_to_change), shows capabilities, flag reasons, and a skip/keep toggle.
RegistryProposals groups dossiers by parent_address or mockup_file, tracks a local skipped set, and renders one batch approve button.
The raw ui:// address is kept as a title tooltip and data-ui-ref, not used as the headline.
standouts: The header encodes RG8 design laws directly in the source file.
A 404 from the backend is treated as 'not built yet' rather than an error.
Only one batch approve button exists; per-entry actions are skip-only.
Grounding is rendered as a discrete badge signal (built/proposed/uncertain/defer).
conventions: React functional components with TypeScript types.
Self-contained controller hook co-located in the same file.
Uses shared kit components (Surface, SectionHead, LaneHead, Badge, EmptyState).
data-ui-ref attributes mark machine loci throughout the UI.
Named exports plus a default export.
Inline styles mixed with CSS class names.
concerns: Skipped state persists across refresh and is not reset when a new batch arrives, which could carry unintended skips if addresses recur.
Fetched data is cast to any before being typed, with no runtime validation against RegistryDossier.
approve sends batch_id even when it is null.
onApprove wraps the async call in void and relies on side-effect notices rather than returned results.
Grouping labels are derived from the first dossier in each group only.
notes: RegistryProposals is the operator-facing review floor for one-batch registry approvals, rendering plain-language dossiers with no-fiction grounding tags and a single commit gate.
questions: What is the exact backend schema for /api/registry/proposals and does it match the frontend's tolerant parsing?
How are parent_address values generated and are they guaranteed stable across batches?
What happens to skipped entries after approval—are they discarded, queued, or logged?
Is the skipped set intended to persist across sessions or only within the current view?
Where are the rgp-* and kit-* CSS rules defined?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (RG8 proposal-batch surface with one approve, plain-language review, no-fiction tags) matches the implemented component.
apparent_themes: Reflects-never-owns (backend truth, local transient state only)
intention_signals: The file is trying to make an opaque registry-generation pipeline legible and controllable for a non-developer operator, turning proposed ui:// addresses into reviewable, grounded, plain-language dossiers with a single explicit consent action.
novelty: The heavy interpretive header that encodes RG8 design laws directly in the source file, and the explicit treatment of 'proposed'/'built'/'uncertain'/'defer' as first-class UI signals rather than hidden metadata.
contribution: It contributes a typed schema for registry dossiers, a self-contained data hook for fetching and approving proposal batches, and a grouped card UI that surfaces grounding, feature mapping, and operator skip/approve actions using shared kit components.
summary_for_embedding: RegistryProposals.tsx is a React TypeScript component that fetches proposed registry dossiers, renders them as plain-language grouped cards with grounding and feature-mapping badges, and provides a single batch-approve action that excludes skipped entries.
intention_for_embedding: It was built to give an operator a transparent, one-consent review surface for proposed ui:// registry entries before they are written back to the registry.

symbols.does: Defines the plain-language how-to trio for a proposed registry entry.
symbols.description: It types the what, what_you_can_do, and how_to_change fields that the surface renders at operator altitude instead of raw JSON.

symbols.does: Defines the shape of a single proposed registry dossier.
symbols.description: It types the proposed ui:// address, label, howto, capabilities, feature mapping, grounding state, optional parent address, mockup file, and flag fields that form the contract between the registry-generation chain and this surface.

symbols.does: Defines the envelope returned by the proposals endpoint.
symbols.description: It types batch_id, the array of dossiers, and an optional error field; batch_id is posted back during approve to identify the batch being consumed.

symbols.does: Self-contained hook that fetches the proposal batch and exposes an approve handler.
symbols.description: It performs GET /api/registry/proposals, tolerates a 404 as an empty honest state, surfaces other errors, and posts {approve:true, batch_id, skip:[...]} to /api/registry/approve while managing loading, approving, and notice state.

symbols.does: Badge that shows whether a dossier maps to a built feature or is proposed/unbuilt.
symbols.description: It renders a 'wire' badge naming the feature id when maps_to_feature is a real id, otherwise an 'await' badge reading 'proposed · not built'.

symbols.does: Badge that renders the dossier's grounding confidence state.
symbols.description: It maps the discrete grounding value to a badge tone: 'sig' for built, 'await' for proposed, and 'fail' when flagged or uncertain, prefixing a flag symbol for scrutiny.

symbols.does: Card component that renders one proposed dossier in plain language.
symbols.description: It displays the represents headline, feature and grounding tags, the howto trio, capability pills, flag reason, and a skip/keep toggle; the raw ui:// address is exposed only via title tooltip and data-ui-ref.

symbols.does: Main surface that groups dossiers and provides the one batch approve gate.
symbols.description: It manages the local skipped set, groups dossiers by parent_address or mockup_file, renders grouped DossierCard rows, shows counts and notices, and exposes a single approve button that commits the kept entries.

symbols.does: Local helper that calls approve with the current skipped set.
symbols.description: It converts the local Set of skipped addresses into an array and invokes the injected or owned approve handler to trigger the RG9 write-back for the kept batch.
